article.title: OpenAI Closes Record $122 Billion Round at $852 Billion Valuation, Opens Door to Retail Investors Ahead of Expected IPO
article.summary: OpenAI's funding round grew from $110 billion to $122 billion with $3 billion from retail investors for the first time, valuing the company at $852 billion as it reports $2 billion in monthly revenue and nears 1 billion weekly ChatGPT users.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

OpenAI completed its record-breaking funding round on March 31, closing at $122 billion in committed capital and an $852 billion post-money valuation, according to [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-31/openai-valued-at-852-billion-after-completing-122-billion-round). The final figure exceeded the $110 billion the company [previously announced](/article/2026-03/06-openai-raises-110-billion-in-largest-private-funding-round-in-history-valued-at-840-billion-as-amazon-becomes-its-second-cloud-partner) in February, reflecting continued investor demand as the company prepares for what is widely expected to be a public listing later this year.

The round's most notable addition is the inclusion of retail investors for the first time in OpenAI's history, with $3 billion raised from individual investors through bank channels, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/openai-not-yet-public-raises-3b-from-retail-investors-in-monster-122b-fund-raise/).

## What We Know

### Investor Breakdown

The round's anchor commitments remain unchanged from the February announcement: Amazon agreed to invest up to $50 billion, while Nvidia and SoftBank each committed $30 billion, as reported by [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-31/openai-valued-at-852-billion-after-completing-122-billion-round). However, $35 billion of Amazon's pledge is contingent on OpenAI either going public or achieving artificial general intelligence, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/openai-not-yet-public-raises-3b-from-retail-investors-in-monster-122b-fund-raise/).

SoftBank co-led the round alongside Andreessen Horowitz, D.E. Shaw Ventures, MGX, TPG, and T. Rowe Price Associates. Microsoft, which has invested more than $13 billion in OpenAI to date, also participated, though OpenAI did not disclose the size of its contribution.

### Retail Access and ETF Inclusion

The $3 billion retail component marks the first time OpenAI has extended participation beyond institutional investors, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/openai-not-yet-public-raises-3b-from-retail-investors-in-monster-122b-fund-raise/). The company is also set to be included in several ETFs managed by ARK Invest, broadening its shareholder base ahead of a potential public offering.

### Revenue and User Growth

OpenAI disclosed that it is generating $2 billion in monthly revenue, up from $13.1 billion in total revenue for the prior year, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/technology/articles/openai-raises-122-billion-boosted-231515284.html). ChatGPT has surpassed 900 million weekly active users with approximately 50 million premium subscribers, and the company's online search usage tripled year-over-year.

### Product Strategy

The company is building what it describes as a "superapp" that combines ChatGPT, web browsing, its Codex coding tool, and AI agent capabilities into a single platform, according to [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/technology/articles/openai-raises-122-billion-boosted-231515284.html). OpenAI has also begun rolling out advertising for non-premium users as an additional revenue stream.

## What We Don't Know

OpenAI has not set a public timeline for its anticipated IPO, though multiple reports indicate it is expected sometime in 2026. The company remains unprofitable despite its rapidly growing revenue, and the full cost structure of its compute infrastructure buildout -- including the Stargate data center project with Oracle and SoftBank -- has not been disclosed.

It is also unclear how the conditional structure of Amazon's $35 billion commitment will be triggered. Whether the AGI clause involves an internal technical benchmark or an external evaluation has not been specified.

## Analysis

The $12 billion increase from the February announcement to the March close reflects both sustained institutional appetite and a deliberate strategy to broaden OpenAI's ownership base before going public. By bringing in retail investors and securing ARK Invest ETF inclusion, OpenAI is building a shareholder constituency that mirrors a publicly traded company -- reducing the friction of an eventual IPO.

The scale of the round is historically unprecedented. At $122 billion, OpenAI's single raise exceeds the total annual venture capital deployed across the entire U.S. startup ecosystem in most years prior to 2021. The $852 billion valuation places the still-private company above the market capitalization of publicly traded firms including Samsung and Walmart.

The advertising rollout for free users signals a shift in OpenAI's business model. For a company that built its consumer brand on a clean, ad-free interface, the introduction of advertising suggests that subscription revenue alone is insufficient to cover the compute costs of serving nearly a billion weekly users. Whether this monetization path alienates users or simply follows the trajectory of every major consumer technology platform remains to be seen.
